Current and Future Developments in International Air Law

Monday, June 15, 2026toThursday, June 18, 2026

To be held between 15-18 June 2026, this intensive four-day executive program offers an advanced executive training concerning the most recent legal, regulatory, and financial frameworks governing the global aviation industry.

Talent Management & Succession Planning (For Managers)

Thursday, June 18, 2026 09:30to12:00

(online session)

Feeling the crunch of budgetary constraints? Concerned for team morale and employee retention? Wondering how you will manage further change? A focus on talent management and succession planning matters more than ever.

By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:

Category:

MIF Lunch & Learn 27: Sales for Non-salespeople

Thursday, June 18, 2026 12:00to13:00

The very nature of entrepreneurship means that company founders will often have to do a little bit of everything: product development, financial planning, human resources, and yes - sales. This can be a challenge for some founders, many of whom often believe that good technology sells itself. But the reality is quite different, and all entrepreneurs will need to learn how to sell so that their businesses will survive. But how can people without sales experience or aptitudes become convincing salespeople?

Read More
Classified as: McGill Innovation Fund
Category:
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Innovation

WS-101 McGill's digital standards

Thursday, June 18, 2026 13:30to15:00

Course Description

McGill's Digital Standards present an overview of the criteria for creating and managing McGill websites and online services including:

  • Best practices
  • Mandatory and legal obligations
  • Industry standards regarding web production and other digital properties

Attendees will learn about McGill's Digital Standards and find out how to achieve the standards on McGill websites.

This course is mandatory for:

Read More
Classified as: Web Services, Web Management System, WMS, IT Training
Category:
  • Services and resources
  • Staff workshops

GCI Research Pipeline Expo

Thursday, June 18, 2026 10:00to15:00

Dear Researchers,

We are pleased to invite you to the GCI Research Pipeline Expo. This event will showcase industry–academic partnerships and highlight research achievements from our core platforms.

Please join us on Thursday, June 18 at 10:00 a.m. in the Goodman Cancer Institute Atrium, with talks beginning at 1:00 p.m.

Thursday, June 18, 2026 13:00to16:00

Prof. Victoria Talwar from The Department of Educational and Counselling Psychology, is proud to announce that Anthony Sciola, a doctoral candidate in School & Applied Child Psychology, will be defending their dissertation on June 18, 2026, at 1PM in Room EDUC 233 and on Zoom (by invitation only).

Wednesday, June 17, 2026 08:30toThursday, June 18, 2026 16:30

Designed and subsidized by the Government of Quebec*, this course is mandatory for Quebec establishments. *Commission des normes, de l'équité, de la santé et de la sécurité du travail [CNESST]

Montreal Summer Choral Academy | Lecture: "Compositional Trends in 20th-century Québécois Choral Music”

Thursday, June 18, 2026 19:00to20:30

Join conductor, educator, and researcher Dr. Gabrielle Gaudreault for an evening exploring the rich and diverse tradition of twentieth-century Québécois choral repertoire. Through discussion, score study, and a public reading session, participants will discover significant works and composers that have shaped Québec's choral landscape. 

This presentation will be given in both English and French.

Read More
Classified as: Free events
Category:
  • Music/Arts/Culture
  • Schulich School of Music
